ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To various Metropolitans and Bishops.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Eusebius of Thessalonica, Urbitius of Dyracchium, Constantius ofMediolanum (Milan), Andrew of Nicopolis, John of Corinth, John of Prima Justiniana, John Cretensi Scoritano, John of Larissa, Marinianus of Ravenna, Januarius of Caralis (Cagliari) in Sardinia, and all the bishops of Sicily.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have taken care to transmit to your Fraternity the law which the most pious Emperor has issued, to the effect that such as are bound by engagements of military service or public liabilities, may not in any case, in order to escape risk of being called to account, assume the condition of ecclesiastics, or become monks: and this I especially press upon you, that such as are involved in secular engagements are not to be received hastily among the clergy of the Church, since, while they live in an ecclesiastical condition no otherwise than they had lived before, they are by no means trying to escape secular affairs, but to change them.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if any such should even seek a monastery, they are by no means to be received unless they have first been absolved from their public liabilities.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, if any from the military order are in haste to become monks, they are not to be received rashly, or until their life has been fully enquired into.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, according to the regular rule, they ought to undergo a probation of three years, and then, God granting it, assume the monastic habit.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And if they have thus been proved and accepted, and are anxious, for the good of their souls, to do penance for the sins they have committed, then, with a view to their heavenly life and gain, monastic profession should not be denied them.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: With respect to this matter also, believe me, the most serene and most Christian Emperor is in every way pacified, and willingly allows the monastic profession of those whom he knows not to be implicated in public liabilities.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The Month of December, first Indiction.
